target.name: RoboTwin
target.founder: Megi Mejdrechová, Ladislav Dvořák, David Polák

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
RoboTwin is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Megi Mejdrechová, Ladislav Dvořák, and David Polák. The company has raised €2.3M in EIC funding. The comparable universe consists of nine peers across robotics and manufacturing: one public company (ticker 277810.KQ) and eight private firms, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

The peer-median valuation method is not applicable to RoboTwin. Although seven same-sector peers exist in robotics and manufacturing, none meet the minimum threshold of three comparable companies when filtered by stage, adjacent-stage proximity, or valuation tier. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given the sector-specific dynamics and capital intensity of robotics.

No triangulated valuation range can be supported from the available data. The analysis lacks sufficient narrow-peer datapoints to generate reliable valuation benchmarks through standard methods. The primary uncertainty stems from the sparsity of seed-stage robotics comparables with disclosed valuations at similar scale and geography. Further valuation work would require either expanded disclosure from the identified private peer set or alternative methodologies (e.g., venture-backed funding multiples, revenue-based approaches if applicable) that depend on data not currently available.
